sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 25 - Guideline for Calculation of Minimum Reserves and Minimum Non-Forfeiture Values for Policies with Guaranteed Increasing Death Benefits Based o.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 25 - Guideline for Calculation of Minimum Reserves and Minimum Non-Forfeiture Values for Policies with Guaranteed Increasing Death Benefits Based on an Index

observedSectionWindows.note: Title, CPI-based annual increase assumptions, low-amount exception, and the closing boundary.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 25 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 26 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Indexed increasing death-benefit guideline
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within pages 1-3 and do not widen into AG 26.

proposedBatches.title: AG 25 indexed increasing death-benefits slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full three-page guideline as one review-only slice because the OCR-noisy wording and exception language should stay together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full three-page guideline as one review-only slice because the OCR-noisy wording and exception language should stay together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, allowance, cap, or required nonforfeiture language as regulatory text when the guideline says it must be followed.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat valuation rate, death-benefit increase terms, threshold amount, and related terms as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat indexed-death-benefit reserve framing as structure until the text clearly states a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the death-benefit adjustments, present-value adjustments, and low-amount exceptions as calculation structure until a duty is explicit.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat formula labels, annual-increase percentages, and policy-value comparisons as context until interpretation is explicit.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat stated valuation assumptions, CPI-based limits, and table-based assumptions as prescribed when the guideline says they are fixed.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Use this label only when the guideline leaves a choice to the insurer or references company-determined treatment.
reviewStandards.scenarioOrStochasticRequirement: Not expected in AG 25; use only if a later page explicitly introduces a scenario requirement.
reviewStandards.assetModelingJudgment: Treat only explicit asset-value or investment-return judgments as asset-modeling judgment.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Consumer Price Index, IRS Section 7702, and the cash value accumulation test as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat title, framing, and explanatory notes as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-3 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the OCR noise, formula-heavy prose, and threshold / exception choices as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 25 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to pages 1-3 only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 25 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Formula-heavy page checks for future indexed-death-benefit guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag25-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-105 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 25 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 25 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 25 planning artifact. Planned batch 105 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
